title: Context: store load/merge
description: Parent epic bd-7a1d2f (label `context`). Precedence: `--context-file` > `--session-file` merge > `NLIR_CONTEXT` env > default file.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: operator longest-match tokenising
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). Longest-match configured ops so `**` beats `*`; ops are non-alphanumeric.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Types: loud coercion errors + list->number error
description: Parent epic bd-957ff4 (label `types`). Failed coercion is a loud error; `list->number` is always an error.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Docs, examples & polish
description: SPEC §all. Child scope (label `docs`): README (usage, config walkthrough, examples) aligned with SPEC; ship a default/example `config.yaml` and default-config scaffolding on first run; error-message & diagnostic polish across lexer/parser/coercion/model failures so shorthand mistakes are legible.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Deterministic evaluator & operator realisation
description: SPEC §Evaluation model, §Modes, §Config operators. Bottom-up evaluator + the non-LLM realisation strategies so the whole `tests:` block passes offline. Child scope (label `eval`): DAG evaluator (operand-first); realisation resolution (`command`/`reduce`/`template`/`join`/`model`) + mode selection; template realisation (`%`, `%0`/`%1`, `%%`); join realisation (variadic); numeric `reduce` builtins (add/sub/mul/div/pow); command realisation (`NLIR_ARGS`, stdout); operand type coercion before apply; list rendering via `_sep` + spread into variadic ops; nullary-pop stack consumption.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Types: deterministic coercions
description: Parent epic bd-957ff4 (label `types`). number<->string, list->string (join `_sep`), string->bool, tried before any LLM call.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: grouping (...) + list literals [a,b,c]
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). `(...)` overrides precedence (preserved in output); `[a,b,c]` list value.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: role-modifier & negative-index disambiguation
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). After `^`/`$`, `* _ /` are role modifiers and leading `-` is a negative index (not subtract).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Project skeleton: Nix flake + Rust `nlir` CLI with crate deps wired
description:
Foundational bead. `SPEC.md` at the repo root is the normative contract for the whole project — read it first; all beads implement it.

Set up the initial project scaffolding so every downstream bead has somewhere to land:

- **Nix flake** managing a Rust CLI, following the same pattern as the other harryaskham Rust CLIs. Use the **`omni-cli`** project as the primary reference for the flake + CLI structure (mature examples also live under `~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/…`, e.g. `tendril`, `kittui`).
- **Rust binary crate `nlir`** with the command-tree scaffolding and stub subcommands from SPEC §CLI surface: `-e`, `parse`, `test`, `repl`, `set`, `get`, `append-message`, `mcp stdio`, `self-update`, `feedback`, plus global flags (`--config`, `--context-file`, `--session-file`, `--mode`, `--model`, `--parallelism`, `--quiet`, `--dry-run`).
- **Wire crate dependencies on the friend-project template stack** (these are configured Cacophony friend projects / sub-crates):
  - `mcp-cli` — gives the MCP server almost for free (`nlir mcp stdio`).
  - `updatable-cli` — `nlir self-update`.
  - `feedback-cli` — `nlir feedback`.
  Reference how `omni-cli` consumes these three crates and mirror that wiring.

Acceptance: `nix build`, `nix flake check`, and `cargo build` are green; `nlir --help` lists the command tree; the three template subcommands are wired (even if their handlers are thin) so later beads only fill in behaviour.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: bare + numeric literal tokens
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). Tokenise bare literals `[a-zA-Z0-9]+` and numeric literals (numeric interpretation in numeric positions such as `^` indices and arithmetic operands).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Modes, output & tracing
description: SPEC §Modes, §Output & tracing. Child scope (label `output`): mode selection (`det`/`llm`, `--mode`, `defaults.mode`); result → stdout; pretty real-time trace of the expansion (ops / LLM calls resolving) → stderr; `--quiet` (stdout only); `--dry-run` (DAG + assembled prompts, no calls).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Types: typed value model
description: Parent epic bd-957ff4 (label `types`). Value enum string/number/bool/list threaded through eval.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: precedence-climbing (Pratt) core
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Pratt over config priority/fixity. Ladder: `^` tightest; prefix unary above binary; `**`>`* /`>`+ -`; `& |`; `?` loose; `=` loosest.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Context store, stack & assignment
description: SPEC §Runtime state, §Builtins (stack + context). The two-namespace runtime: JSON context KV + internal stack. Child scope (label `context`): context load/merge (`NLIR_CONTEXT` env / `--context-file` / default `~/.config/nlir/context.json`); system keys (`_messages`, `_sep`=`\n`, `_cache`=true); stack machine (`;` push, `$`/`$N` peek, nullary-pop); `key=RHS` assignment (literal key, immediate write-through); `$name` read + greedy eval-time resolution; `"…"` `$name` interpolation.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Config: OS-env interpolation at load
description: Parent epic bd-b342fd (label `config`). SPEC §Prompt templating (layer 1). Interpolate real OS env vars `$FOO`/`${FOO}` at config load, e.g. `api_key: $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. `NLIR_`-prefixed vars are engine-internal and must NOT be resolved here.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: quoted literals + POSIX escapes
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). SPEC §Whitespace/quoting. `'…'` raw (no escapes/interp), `"…"` escapes-processed; POSIX escapes `\n \t \  \\ \" \'` produce literal chars incl. whitespace.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Types: coercion caching under _cache
description: Parent epic bd-957ff4 (label `types`). Cache coercions keyed on (text, target-type, model) when `_cache`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] MCP / self-update / feedback plumbing
description: SPEC §Implementation / project shape. Fill in the template-stack subcommands the skeleton wired. Child scope (label `plumbing`): `nlir mcp stdio` MCP server exposing the CLI surface (mcp-cli); `nlir self-update` (updatable-cli); `nlir feedback` (feedback-cli). Mirror the `omni-cli` usage of each crate.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Message indexing (^ role views)
description: SPEC §Builtins (Message indexing). `^` as sugar over `$_messages`, role-filtered. Child scope (label `messages`): role views `^`=assistant / `^_`=user / `^*`=all / `^/`=system (config-driven role mapping); index resolution where indices are expressions coerced to number, negatives from the end; range `M^N` (and variants) with contents joined by `_sep`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Type system & coercion
description: SPEC §Types & coercion. Tiny type system (`string`/`number`/`bool`/`list`) with per-position coercion. Child scope (label `types`): typed value model; deterministic coercions (number↔string, list→string via `_sep`, string→bool); LLM coercion fallback ("interpret as type T" with structured-output schema, config-driven under `types:`); loud coercion errors + `list→number` is an error; coercion caching under `_cache`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Sessions & Pi harness integration
description: SPEC §CLI (`--session-file`), §Example config (`sessions:`), §Pi plugin. Child scope (label `sessions`): `--session-file` parsing driven by `sessions.<agent>` config (format, `keep_roles`, `drop_tool_messages`, role/content fields) — parse Pi session JSON/JSONL into `_messages`; merge session-derived `_messages` into the effective context; Pi drop-in plugin (leading `|` prompt convention → expand first; `append-message` per turn; pipe shorthand into a long-lived `nlir repl --raw`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: non-semantic whitespace stripping
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). SPEC §Whitespace. Strip spaces/tabs/newlines between tokens (non-semantic) so an expression can be laid out as a multi-line program.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Config: discovery & load (default path + --config)
description: Parent epic bd-b342fd (label `config`). Load `~/.config/nlir/config.yaml` by default; honour `--config PATH`; clear diagnostics on missing/malformed config.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Execution graph & parallelism
description: SPEC §Execution graph & parallelism. Concurrent evaluation of independent DAG nodes with safety. Child scope (label `parallelism`): DAG scheduler (independent nodes concurrent, `--parallelism` default 8); context-write serialization safety (`=`); backtick forced-serial subtree (whole RHS, sibling-parallel); subcall dedupe/cache keyed on `(op, mode, model, operand-texts)` under `_cache`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Config: serde schema structs for the full config tree
description: Parent epic bd-b342fd (label `config`). SPEC §Example config.yaml. Define serde types for the full config tree: `defaults`, `models`, `prompts`, `operators`, `context`, `sessions`, `types`, `tests`. Foundation the rest of config builds on.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Parser & DAG construction
description: SPEC §Mental model, §Grammar & parsing. Precedence-climbing (Pratt) parser driven by config priority/fixity, producing a DAG of statements. Child scope (label `parser`): Pratt core; prefix/postfix/infix + arity; variadic flattening; mixfix unification (infix / prefix-on-list / postfix-on-list / nullary-pop); grouping `(…)` + list literals `[a,b,c]`; statement split `;` + DAG build; backtick serial-marker (low-precedence prefix over whole RHS); `nlir parse` AST/DAG dump.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: statement split ; + DAG build
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Split `;` statements; assemble the evaluation DAG (independent subtrees identifiable).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Config loading, schema & validation
description: SPEC §Modes, §Types & coercion, §Example config.yaml. The `~/.config/nlir/config.yaml` layer that defines the whole language (models, prompts, operators, context, sessions, types, defaults, tests). Child scope (label `config`): serde schema structs; config discovery + load with `--config` override and default path; OS-env interpolation at load (`$FOO`/`${FOO}`); validation (required fields, unknown keys, operator op/arity/fixity sanity, reserved-sigil collisions); defaults resolution (mode/model/parallelism, context `_sep`/`_cache`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] LLM model backends & prompt pipeline
description: SPEC §Prompt templating pipeline, §Example config (models/prompts). The `llm`-mode realisation. Child scope (label `llm`): model resolution (alias→model, default model); `anthropic_messages` backend (direct HTTP, `json_schema` structured output, `result_field`); `command` backend (subprocess, `format` json/text, `result_field`); prompt fragments (`prompts:` env-named); `%` operand substitution (arity-1 `<text>`, variadic `<text n=k>`, `%%`); `${NLIR_*}` assembly (`NLIR_PROMPT` + system/structured/unstructured + `NLIR_ARGS`); result extraction (json `result_field` vs raw stdout text).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Test runner
description: SPEC §Example config (`tests:`). Child scope (label `testrunner`): `nlir test` executes the config `tests:` block (each entry: `mode`, `expr`, `expected`, optional inline `context`/`_messages`); run in a temporary context, compare output, report pass/fail with diffs; suitable as the offline `det`-mode regression gate.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] CLI surface & context subcommands
description: SPEC §CLI surface. Flesh out the command tree scaffolded by the skeleton. Child scope (label `cli`): top-level `-e` eval + flags (`--mode`, `--model`, `--parallelism`, `--config`); context-source precedence (`--context-file` › `--session-file` › `NLIR_CONTEXT` env › default file); `set` (key replacement, JSON-object merge form); `get`; `append-message` (`--role`, append to `_messages`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: backtick serial-marker
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Low-precedence prefix capturing whole RHS as a forced-serial subtree.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Lexer / tokeniser
description: SPEC §Whitespace/escapes/quoting, §Grammar (Tokens). Turn shorthand text into a token stream. Child scope (label `lexer`): non-semantic whitespace stripping between tokens; bare `[a-zA-Z0-9]+` and numeric literals; quoted literals `'…'` (raw) / `"…"` (escapes processed) with POSIX escapes (`\n \t \  \\ \" \'`); operator longest-match (`**` before `*`); builtin sigils `; $ ^ = [ ] , ( )` + backtick and sub-forms (`$name`/`$N`, `^`/`^_`/`^*`/`^/`, `key=`); role-modifier and negative-index disambiguation after `^`/`$`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: variadic flattening
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Flatten adjacent same-op infix into one n-ary node; parens force nesting.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] REPL
description: SPEC §CLI surface (repl). Long-lived pipe for coding agents. Child scope (label `repl`): repl loop (one expression per submission, trailing `\` continuation, re-read context file each eval so external `append-message` writes show up); `--raw` output (result-only per line); `:cmd` meta-commands equivalent to `nlir cmd` (`:set`/`:get`/`:append-message`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: nlir parse AST/DAG dump
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). `nlir parse 'EXPR'` prints the AST/DAG only.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: builtin sigils + sub-forms
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). Sigils `; $ ^ = [ ] , ( )` + backtick, sub-forms `$name`/`$N`, `^`/`^_`/`^*`/`^/`, `key=`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Config: validation (fields, unknown keys, sigil collisions)
description: Parent epic bd-b342fd (label `config`). Validate: required fields present, unknown keys rejected, operator `op`/`arity`/`fixity` sanity, operator sigils don't collide with reserved builtin sigils.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Config: defaults resolution (mode/model/parallelism, _sep/_cache)
description: Parent epic bd-b342fd (label `config`). Resolve `defaults.mode`/`model`/`parallelism` and context defaults `_sep` (`\n`) / `_cache` (true), with CLI flags overriding config.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: mixfix unification
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Unify `a&b`, `a&b&c`, `&[a,b,c]`, `[a,b,c]&`, nullary-pop `&`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Types: LLM coercion fallback
description: Parent epic bd-957ff4 (label `types`). Per-type `types:` model+prompt with `{result:T}` schema when deterministic parse fails.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: prefix/postfix/infix + arity
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Fixity + arity; `!a&b&c`=`(!a)&b&c`; postfix `?` binds leftward.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Context: system keys + defaults
